jokes.text: I am Dwanta Claus, and during this time of the year, I like to rip the sleeves off my jacket and put on a hat with a bell
jokes.setup: Character introducing himself as 'Dwanta Claus'
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of ripping sleeves off and putting on a bell hat as Christmas preparation
jokes.explanation: Strong character-based humor that establishes Dwayne's unique take on Christmas. The 'Dwanta Claus' persona is genuinely creative and the physical comedy description is vivid.

jokes.text: I love Christmas because it's all about the three G's, gathering, giving, and getting loose on eggnog
jokes.setup: Setting up a traditional 'three G's' formula about Christmas values
jokes.punchline: Third G subverts expectation with 'getting loose on eggnog' instead of something wholesome
jokes.explanation: Classic rule-of-three structure with good misdirection. Economy is excellent - maximum funny per word.

jokes.text: They're from the Jane Seymour for Mervyn's collection
jokes.setup: Proud presentation of special slippers
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity of 'Jane Seymour for Mervyn's collection' - a discount department store line
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail that reveals both pretension and modest means. The Jane Seymour/Mervyn's combo is beautifully specific.

jokes.text: as much as I love the Radish Baby that you got me last year...
jokes.setup: Polite opening about appreciating last year's gift
jokes.punchline: 'Radish Baby' - an absurd toy name that clearly wasn't what he wanted
jokes.explanation: 'Radish Baby' is wonderfully absurd and specific - sounds like a knockoff toy. Sets up the gift-giving theme perfectly.

jokes.text: That doll is not right
jokes.setup: Response to mention of the Radish Baby
jokes.punchline: Deadpan acknowledgment that the gift was clearly wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement that acknowledges the absurdity. Economy is excellent - four words do the job.

jokes.text: Why does he have muscles on his body but not on his face?
jokes.setup: Looking at Skeletor action figure picture
jokes.punchline: Child's logical observation about the anatomical inconsistency of Skeletor
jokes.explanation: Genuinely funny child logic applied to toy design. Very character-specific observation that a kid would make.

jokes.text: But it's on hold under your name!
jokes.setup: Dad says he'll pass info to Santa
jokes.punchline: Kid accidentally reveals he knows his dad is Santa by mentioning the hold
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe moment where the kid accidentally breaks the Santa illusion. Good character work showing his practical nature.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, just between us, I kind of hate that Radish Baby
jokes.setup: Private confession to his father
jokes.punchline: Blunt honesty about hating the weird gift
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces the absurdity of the original gift. Kid's bluntness is endearing.

jokes.text: Adults give gifts to kids, not the other way around
jokes.setup: Father suggesting he get mom a gift
jokes.punchline: Kid's logical but selfish interpretation of how gift-giving works
jokes.explanation: Perfect kid logic that's both selfish and reasonable from a child's perspective. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I see. So you don't love your mother
jokes.setup: Kid says adults give gifts to kids
jokes.punchline: Rocky's dramatic leap to questioning his love for his mother
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that shows Rocky's dramatic parenting style. The logical leap is absurd but delivered with conviction.

jokes.text: I do... not wanna know how they get oil out of them babies
jokes.setup: Rocky saying they should make their own baby oil
jokes.punchline: Andre's literal interpretation of 'baby oil' coming from actual babies
jokes.explanation: Classic literal interpretation joke. Not highly original but well-executed with Andre's character voice.

jokes.text: You are like a little Père Noel
jokes.setup: Dewey wanting to give gifts
jokes.punchline: Andre's French reference to Father Christmas
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Andre's French background. Gentle humor rather than big laugh.

jokes.text: You should get her a hot tub, a big one, you know, one that we can all fit in
jokes.setup: Kid asking for gift advice for mom
jokes.punchline: Rocky's suggestion is clearly for his own benefit
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Rocky's self-serving nature disguised as helpful advice.

jokes.text: Last year I got us matching diamond tennis bracelets
jokes.setup: Discussing jewelry gifts
jokes.punchline: Rocky got himself matching jewelry with his wife
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Rocky's vanity and poor gift-giving instincts. The matching aspect makes it even more self-serving.

jokes.text: Oh, make sure it's a big one, you know, one that we could all fit in
jokes.setup: Hot tub suggestion seeming to win
jokes.punchline: Rocky repeats his self-serving requirement
jokes.explanation: Good running gag that reinforces Rocky's priorities. The repetition drives home his selfishness.

jokes.text: I wish there was a toy catalogue for adults
jokes.setup: Struggling to find mom a gift
jokes.punchline: Kid's logical wish for an adult version of what works for kids
jokes.explanation: Great kid logic that actually makes perfect sense. Sets up the mall punchline nicely.

jokes.text: Dewey, you just described a mall
jokes.setup: Kid's elaborate description of needing an adult toy catalogue
jokes.punchline: Rocky's simple realization that this already exists
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline with excellent economy. The misdirection works beautifully - kid overthinking something simple.

jokes.text: Oh, candy. No. Gotta stay focused. I'm here for Mom
jokes.setup: At the mall looking for mom's gift
jokes.punchline: Kid immediately getting distracted by what he wants
jokes.explanation: Realistic kid behavior that's relatable and funny. Shows his struggle between selflessness and natural kid impulses.

jokes.text: Well, aren't you a little big to sit on Santa's lap?
jokes.setup: Dwayne approaching mall Santa
jokes.punchline: Santa's reasonable observation about age appropriateness
jokes.explanation: Standard observational humor. Functional but not particularly creative or memorable.

jokes.text: Me. Anything else?
jokes.setup: Santa asking what his mom likes
jokes.punchline: Kid's honest, simple answer that she likes him
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and sweet character moment. The honesty and simplicity make it endearing and funny.

jokes.text: I got nothing
jokes.setup: Trying to think of what else mom likes
jokes.punchline: Honest admission of complete failure
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective. The blunt honesty is endearing and relatable.

jokes.text: even if you just slap googly eyes on a coconut
jokes.setup: Santa saying any gift will make mom happy
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific example of a terrible gift
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup for the episode's central running gag. The specificity is what makes it funny - not just 'anything' but this exact absurd thing.

jokes.text: Might have overdone it with the glitter paint
jokes.setup: Making the googly-eyed coconut
jokes.punchline: Kid's understatement about excessive decorating
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy that shows kid enthusiasm. The understatement suggests it's hilariously over-decorated.

jokes.text: Ah! No! No!
jokes.setup: Coconut presumably drying
jokes.punchline: Disaster strikes the homemade gift
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment that escalates the stakes. The repeated 'No!' suggests mounting disaster.

jokes.text: Were you doing crafts? No! I was just... organizing the craft supplies
jokes.setup: Mom notices obvious craft activity
jokes.punchline: Kid's terrible lie about 'organizing' when caught
jokes.explanation: Classic kid caught-in-the-act moment. The overexplanation makes the lie even more obvious and funny.

jokes.text: Someone put a glue stick inside of the Magic Marker tray. People are animals
jokes.setup: Explaining why he was 'organizing' craft supplies
jokes.punchline: Elaborate explanation ending with dramatic condemnation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from simple lie to philosophical outrage. 'People are animals' is a great overreaction to minor organization issue.

jokes.text: Her... her mango shipment was late so she asked me to go to the store and get her some
jokes.setup: Mom asking about his errand for grandma
jokes.punchline: Absurd lie about grandma's mango shipment being late
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. The specificity of 'mango shipment' makes it funnier than a generic lie.

jokes.text: $15? What are these made out of, gold doubloons?
jokes.setup: Shocked at slipper price
jokes.punchline: Kid's dramatic questioning using pirate currency reference
jokes.explanation: Good use of anachronistic currency reference. Shows kid's dramatic tendencies and sticker shock.

jokes.text: You look too young to be here, but too old to be lost
jokes.setup: Encountering confused-looking kid in store
jokes.punchline: Perfectly captures the awkward age/situation assessment
jokes.explanation: Excellent observation that perfectly captures the liminal space of pre-teen shopping. Great economy and specificity.

jokes.text: Look, Brig-itty. Brigitte. Bresheet. No.
jokes.setup: Kid trying to pronounce saleswoman's name
jokes.punchline: Multiple failed attempts getting progressively worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing kid's struggle with pronunciation. Each attempt gets more wrong, which is realistic and funny.

jokes.text: I'm a businessman, and you're a businesswoman
jokes.setup: Kid attempting to negotiate with store clerk
jokes.punchline: Formal business language from a child
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between kid's age and formal business approach. Shows his developing personality.

jokes.text: this is a Mervyn's. We don't haggle
jokes.setup: Kid trying to negotiate
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact statement about department store protocol
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that highlights the absurdity of trying to haggle at a department store.

jokes.text: so I made her a stupid coconut with googly eyes, and then I dropped it on her Jane Seymour slippers
jokes.setup: Explaining his Christmas disaster to Santa
jokes.punchline: Connecting multiple established elements into one catastrophe
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that connects established elements. The chain of disasters is both believable and funny.

jokes.text: she's literally a Christmas angel
jokes.setup: Explaining how bad he feels about ruining Christmas
jokes.punchline: Dramatic description of mom's Christmas perfection
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing kid's dramatic tendencies and genuine guilt. The hyperbole is endearing.

jokes.text: Back to socks like the rest of us
jokes.setup: Mom can't find her special slippers
jokes.punchline: Rocky's matter-of-fact acceptance of fashion downgrade
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan observation about fashion hierarchy. 'Like the rest of us' suggests socks are peasant footwear.

jokes.text: So my baby will always have some shade
jokes.setup: Talking about umbrella hat gift
jokes.punchline: Treating ridiculous hat as practical parenting tool
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that shows mom finding practical use for ridiculous gift. Shows her loving nature.

jokes.text: God damn, I could've just been making coconuts
jokes.setup: Seeing mom's genuine happiness with simple gift
jokes.punchline: Kid's realization he overthought the whole thing
jokes.explanation: Excellent realization moment that captures the episode's theme perfectly. The mild profanity from a kid adds to the authenticity.

jokes.text: Sorry, Dad. These coconuts are all mine
jokes.setup: Dad asking to share credit for coconut tradition
jokes.punchline: Kid's possessive response to his special tradition
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dwayne's possessiveness about his special thing with mom.

jokes.text: I love how you just assume that I have nothing to do all day and free to come to work with you and go to a party
jokes.setup: Dad telling him he's coming to work and a party
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic complaint about assumptions
jokes.explanation: Good teenage sarcasm that sets up the punchline. The complaint is justified but we know he has nothing planned.

jokes.text: Do you have something to do today? No.
jokes.setup: Kid's sarcastic complaint about assumptions
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission that the assumption was correct
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of the previous complaint. Excellent economy - one word does all the work.

jokes.text: Which I'm pretty sure permanently stained my hands
jokes.setup: Mentioning cleaning with Christmas tree hand soaps
jokes.punchline: Concern about permanent staining from festive soap
jokes.explanation: Good detail about the hazards of festive cleaning supplies. Shows attention to absurd consequences.

jokes.text: Damn, that's not a little anything
jokes.setup: Boss saying she has 'a little something'
jokes.punchline: Kid's blunt assessment of gift size
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about gift-giving euphemisms. Kid's directness cuts through politeness.

jokes.text: We got you something too, uh, just left it at home. We'll bring it to the party
jokes.setup: Receiving unexpected gift
jokes.punchline: Obvious lie about reciprocal gift
jokes.explanation: Classic awkward social lie. The pause and 'uh' make it obviously improvised.

jokes.text: You don't gift up
jokes.setup: Dewey saying they need to get boss a gift
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact statement about workplace hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Great encapsulation of workplace dynamics in three words. The 'up' direction metaphor is perfect.

jokes.text: The man owns a T. Rex skull. The hell can I buy him?
jokes.setup: Asking if he ever got Vince a gift
jokes.punchline: Extreme example of why gifting up is impossible
jokes.explanation: Perfect example that illustrates the impossibility of the task. T-Rex skull is absurd but believable for a wrestling promoter.

jokes.text: It's free, ain't it?
jokes.setup: Rocky excited about radio contest
jokes.punchline: Andre's practical assessment of contest value
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andre logic - simple, practical, and optimistic. The grammar adds to his character.

jokes.text: $300? What, does it grant wishes?
jokes.setup: Seeing expensive item price
jokes.punchline: Hyperbolic questioning of price justification
jokes.explanation: Good sticker shock humor. The wish-granting comparison is absurd but effective.

jokes.text: You wash... I'll die. Instead of 'I'll dry'
jokes.setup: Finding dish towel with text
jokes.punchline: Morbid misprint on kitchen towel
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag based on typo. The explanation makes it clear and the contrast between intended/actual is funny.

jokes.text: Stealing? At Christmastime? Certainly not anymore
jokes.setup: Ata suggesting they could get something without paying
jokes.punchline: Rocky's moral outrage that reveals past Christmas stealing
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal that subverts expectation. 'Certainly not anymore' implies he used to steal at Christmas, which is darkly funny.

jokes.text: This is a regift? She didn't put any thought into this. But all she did was wrap up something she didn't want. That's not even a gift. That's decorated trash
jokes.setup: Realizing the ice cream maker was used
jokes.punchline: Building outrage ending with 'decorated trash'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with excellent final line. 'Decorated trash' is a brilliant description of regifting that becomes quotable.

jokes.text: And that's exactly what we're gonna give her
jokes.setup: Dwayne's outrage about decorated trash
jokes.punchline: Rocky's decision to reciprocate with same energy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Rocky's petty side. The decision to match the slight is very human.

jokes.text: Aw, you gotta tinkle winkle?
jokes.setup: Andre presumably needing bathroom during contest
jokes.punchline: Radio host's infantilizing baby talk
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor from radio personality's inappropriate baby talk to adult. Shows the tackiness of the contest.

jokes.text: Rocky Johnson! Can I get your autograph? Hell yeah
jokes.setup: Andre loses contest due to distraction
jokes.punchline: Fan recognition causes the loss but Rocky's enthusiastic response
jokes.explanation: Good irony where success causes failure. Rocky's immediate 'Hell yeah' shows his priorities.

jokes.text: Price of fame
jokes.setup: Andre losing contest due to Rocky's fan
jokes.punchline: Rocky's philosophical acceptance of unintended consequence
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy with three words. Shows Rocky's ability to rationalize anything as part of celebrity life.

jokes.text: Well, maybe you could spill some red wine on it and give it to someone else
jokes.setup: Giving dish towel as regift
jokes.punchline: Suggesting she continue the regifting cycle
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that suggests creating the same problem they discovered. Shows Dwayne's petty but logical revenge.

jokes.text: I almost got it engraved to say 'Never shuts up'... But it's Christmas
jokes.setup: Giving flask as Secret Santa gift
jokes.punchline: Revealing the mean engraving he considered but restraint for holiday
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Stone Cold's mean streak tempered by Christmas spirit. The restraint makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Well, the coconut is a family tradition. I make one for my mom every year. It used to be he was the only gift, but now he brings other gifts with him
jokes.setup: Mick asking about the coconut
jokes.punchline: Elaborate mythology around the coconut gift-bearer
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that expands the coconut lore. The idea that the coconut 'brings' other gifts is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Actually, this time tomorrow you'll be on your layover in Charlotte, but it's a nice airport
jokes.setup: Telling Mick about his trip timing
jokes.punchline: Overly specific travel logistics ending with weak airport praise
jokes.explanation: Good travel humor about the reality vs. expectation. The Charlotte airport detail is nicely specific and the praise is hilariously weak.

jokes.text: I'm just not a huge fan of flying over water
jokes.setup: Being offered Bahamas trip
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific fear for tough wrestler
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between tough guy persona and specific fear. Shows everyone has something that scares them.

jokes.text: I'm not a robe guy. Thanks to my father who could never find the belt for his
jokes.setup: Refusing to trade for robe
jokes.punchline: Specific childhood trauma about father's robe usage
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific detail that explains an adult preference through childhood experience. The visual is both funny and relatable.

jokes.text: Well, this is every tack I've ever pulled out of my back after a match, fused together to form a life-sized Tori Amos
jokes.setup: Showing Dwayne something in his collection
jokes.punchline: Bizarre art piece combining wrestling pain with music fandom
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurdist humor. The combination of wrestling violence and Tori Amos fandom is so specific and unexpected it's genius.

jokes.text: Has Tori seen this? And if so, is she flattered or is this a restraining order situation?
jokes.setup: Learning about the Tori Amos thumbtack sculpture
jokes.punchline: Practical questions about celebrity reaction to bizarre tribute
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that escalates the absurdity. The restraining order possibility makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Heels can't do charity events for kids. Good guys only
jokes.setup: Dwayne wanting to help with toy drive
jokes.punchline: Wrestling kayfabe logic applied to real-world charity
jokes.explanation: Great application of wrestling logic to charity work. Shows how the business affects real life in absurd ways.

jokes.text: Oh, no! It's the Grinch!
jokes.setup: Dwayne appearing at children's event
jokes.punchline: Kids' terrified reaction to Dwayne's Grinch costume
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the heel charity restriction. The kids' genuine fear makes Dwayne the perfect villain.

jokes.text: before Stone Cold sticks his mistletoe right up your chimney
jokes.setup: Threatening the Grinch
jokes.punchline: Christmas-themed violent threat
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific threat that combines Christmas imagery with Stone Cold's violent persona.

jokes.text: This one's a pepper mill for my good friend, Stanley Tucci. Merry Christmas, Tooch
jokes.setup: Dwayne showing off another gift
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific gift and casual nickname for famous actor
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity name-drop with the casual 'Tooch' nickname. Pepper mill is an oddly specific gift choice.

jokes.text: But hold the sandwiches, and hold the cookies. That's right. Dwanta's gettin' loaded
jokes.setup: Listing what to leave for Dwanta Claus
jokes.punchline: Eliminating food to focus on alcohol consumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to opening persona with adult twist on Santa traditions. 'Dwanta's gettin' loaded' is highly quotable.
